title: issue cross-compiling podman for armv6..
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Having gotten tired of docker using up the little memory on my rapsberry pi Model B, i decided to try podman.  I've gotten this to build previously but when trying to use rootless mode, i seemed to have been affected by [this issue](https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/5314) so i git pull'd to get the latest and tried a 2nd time to uncompile, this time unsuccessfully.. Now having trouble building podman..  I get the following compile errors:

```
root@3e19a2cfde29:/src/github.com/containers/libpod# make
GO111MODULE=on go build -mod=vendor  -gcflags 'all=-trimpath=/src/github.com/containers/libpod' -asmflags 'all=-trimpath=/src/github.com/containers/libpod' -ldflags '-X github.com/containers/libpod/libpod/define.gitCommit=f07e18f05c07026e4544788a1359122c81adcde7 -X github.com/containers/libpod/libpod/define.buildInfo=1583502613 -X github.com/containers/libpod/libpod/config._installPrefix=/usr/local -X github.com/containers/libpod/libpod/config._etcDir=/etc -extldflags ""' -tags "apparmor   selinux systemd exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper seccomp varlink" -o bin/podman github.com/containers/libpod/cmd/podman
# github.com/containers/libpod/libpod
libpod/container_api.go:302:21: constant -2147483649 overflows int
libpod/container_api.go:346:24: constant -2147483649 overflows int
libpod/oci_conmon_linux.go:1560:7: constant -2147483649 overflows int
Makefile:184: recipe for target 'bin/podman' failed
make: *** [bin/podman] Error 2
```

i'm leveraging the procedure from [this page](https://computingforgeeks.com/how-to-install-podman-on-debian/) for what dependencies are needed, and additionally, output of `go version`:
```
go version go1.13.8 linux/arm
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. setup cross-compile environment (to be able to build armv6 on x86_64), on x86_64 linux host, setup bin_fmt by launching [multiarch/qemu-user-static:register](https://libraries.io/github/multiarch/qemu-user-static) image in privileged mode on host (as documented [here](https://www.ecliptik.com/Cross-Building-and-Running-Multi-Arch-Docker-Images/))

2. for the environment, pull/run resin/rpi-raspbian:latest image and in there, follow [process for building podman on Debian 9/10](https://computingforgeeks.com/how-to-install-podman-on-debian/).

**Describe the results you received:**

3. at the compile stage, the overflows int errors are encountered

**Describe the results you expected:**

I would like the 

**Additional information you deem important:**

I had a similar issue previously a few weeks ago, but was able to (hackishly) get around it by casting the offending type:

```
root@3e19a2cfde29:/src/github.com/containers/libpod# git diff
diff --git a/vendor/github.com/opencontainers/selinux/go-selinux/selinux_linux.go b/vendor/github.com/opencontainers/selinux/go-selinux/selinux_linux.go
index 0e97a077..0cf0e396 100644
--- a/vendor/github.com/opencontainers/selinux/go-selinux/selinux_linux.go
+++ b/vendor/github.com/opencontainers/selinux/go-selinux/selinux_linux.go
@@ -115,7 +115,7 @@ func verifySELinuxfsMount(mnt string) bool {
      return false
   }  

-  if buf.Type != unix.SELINUX_MAGIC {
+  if uint32(buf.Type) != uint32(unix.SELINUX_MAGIC) {
      return false
   }  
   if (buf.Flags & stRdOnly) != 0 {
```

(as i'm not using selinux, i felt this hack would not be problematic), unfortunately a similar trick did not work as i'd need to do extensive casting and handling, so opening up this bug request instead..

however, from when i did get podman previously compiled: 

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
pi@raspberrypi:~$ podman version
WARN[0000] The cgroups manager is set to systemd but there is no systemd user session available 
WARN[0000] For using systemd, you may need to login using an user session 
WARN[0000] Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 1000` (possibly as root) 
WARN[0000] Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs

RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.8
Git Commit:         18dcb84d641f4e8ae7fa31fa446d9461e0c915ab-dirty
Built:              Mon Feb 24 18:05:18 2020
OS/Arch:            linux/arm
```

```

      commit: 3e425f80a8c931f88e6d94a8c831b9d5aa481657

  SwapFree: 0

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
[not applicable - being compiled from git repo]
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

While the target environment is Raspbian, which is Debian9-based (stretch), where i am getting this issue is while cross-compiling (using the [multiarch](https://github.com/multiarch/qemu-user-static) project), in a docker container that emulates my target environment, hosted on a regular x86_64 linux host, using the docker image `resin/rpi-raspbian:latest`.

comments.body: Can you try master? I think we may have fixed this with a revendor of containers/selinux
Yes, this has been fixed in master.

selinux has been fixed in master, but what i am opening _this_ issue about is 

```
libpod/container_api.go:302:21: constant -2147483649 overflows int
libpod/container_api.go:346:24: constant -2147483649 overflows int
libpod/oci_conmon_linux.go:1560:7: constant -2147483649 overflows int
```

if you closed it because of selinux is fixed then that is not what this issue is about, please re-open..
Apologies for missing that! Repoened the issue.

Additional information...

Using a Raspberry PI 4 with ubuntu 19.10 64-bit, Built on-board, not cross compiled.

`make BUILDTAGS='seccomp apparmor'`

```
$ bin/podman info --debug

I believe this is fixed in master.
confirmed, now cleanly built off master as of last night
